description:
You are managing a Type 1 Diabetes patient using AndroidAPS who is about to begin a 60-minute exercise session. To prevent exercise-induced hypoglycemia (a well-documented clinical risk — see Riddell et al., Lancet Diabetes Endocrinol, 2017), you need to set a Temporary Target in AndroidAPS.

The app is currently showing the main overview screen with tabs (OVERVIEW, ACTIONS, PROFILE) at the top. There may be red warning banners about permissions — you can scroll past or dismiss these.

Using the AndroidAPS Temp Target feature:
1. Open the Temp Target dialog (tap the target area on the overview, or go to the ACTIONS tab and use a Careportal option, or use the 3-dot menu > Treatments)
2. Select or configure an 'Exercise' temporary target
3. Set the target blood glucose to 140 mg/dL
4. Set the duration to 60 minutes
5. Confirm and activate the temporary target

The temporary target should appear on the main overview graph once set.
